senses.definition: the process that involves teaching and learning, particularly at a school, university, or college

examples.x: Education is the key to unlocking opportunities for personal and professional growth.
The government invested in improving access to quality education for all children.
Online education has become increasingly popular due to its flexibility and accessibility.
She dedicated her career to advocating for inclusive education for students with disabilities.

senses.definition: to teach someone, often within a school or university setting

examples.x: Schools exist to educate children.
The university aims to educate future leaders.
Teachers work hard to educate their students.
The program helps educate young people about health.
She was educated at a prestigious university.

senses.definition: intended to provide knowledge or facilitate learning

examples.x: The educational video series covers various topics in history for middle school students.
The educational field trip to the zoo allowed students to learn about different animal species.
Online educational platforms offer courses on a wide range of subjects, from photography to computer programming.

senses.definition: having received a good education

examples.x: As an educated individual, she possessed a strong foundation of knowledge and critical thinking skills acquired through years of study and exploration.
Educated individuals are equipped with the tools to analyze information critically and make informed decisions.
The educated population is better equipped to adapt to changes in the workplace and society.
Educated citizens play a vital role in building and maintaining a democratic society by participating in informed decision-making.

senses.definition: related to education, particularly higher education

examples.x: Academic institutions offer a variety of courses and programs designed to provide students with a comprehensive education.
Academic journals are peer-reviewed publications that contribute to the dissemination of scholarly research within various disciplines.
Writing an academic essay involves synthesizing information from multiple sources and presenting a coherent argument.

senses.definition: a task given to a student to do

examples.x: The teacher handed out a challenging assignment on algebraic equations.
Completing the history assignment required researching multiple sources.
She spent hours working on her science assignment, conducting experiments and writing up her findings.
He struggled with the math assignment but sought help from a tutor.
The English assignment involved writing a persuasive essay on a controversial topic.

senses.definition: tasks that are given to students to do in class, and not at home

examples.x: The teacher assigned a group discussion as part of the classwork for today.
Students were asked to solve math problems on the board for their classwork.
Completing the worksheet was part of the classwork for the lesson on fractions.
The classwork involved conducting experiments in the science laboratory.
Completing the reading comprehension exercises was part of the daily classwork.

senses.definition: an area of land in which a university, college, or school, along with all their buildings, are situated

examples.x: The campus of the university is spread out over 100 acres of land.
Students often gather in the quad on campus to study or socialize.
There are several dining options available on campus for students and faculty.
The campus is known for its beautiful landscaping and green spaces.
Security patrols the campus to ensure the safety of students and staff.

senses.definition: a certificate given to someone who has completed a course of study

examples.x: She proudly displayed her diploma on the wall of her office.
Graduates received their diplomas during the commencement ceremony.
He earned his high school diploma after completing four years of coursework.
After completing the apprenticeship program, he received a diploma in carpentry.
The diploma serves as proof of completion of the educational program and can be used for employment or further education.

senses.definition: classes for adults to finish their education, held in the evening or over the Internet

examples.x: The university's evening classes cater exclusively to adult education.
The nonprofit organization focuses its resources on promoting adult education in underserved communities.
He decided to pursue adult education to earn his high school diploma.
Adult education plays a crucial role in empowering individuals to enhance their skills and knowledge.
Many adults return to school through adult education to acquire new qualifications or advance in their careers.

senses.definition: education at a university or similar educational institution that grants one an academic degree at the end

examples.x: Higher education can be expensive, but it often leads to better job opportunities.
She decided to continue her studies in higher education after completing her undergraduate degree.
Higher education institutions often offer scholarships to help students afford tuition.
Many people attend higher education to gain qualifications that are required for certain professions.
Higher education is a long-term investment that can lead to personal and professional growth.

senses.definition: used by or belonging to only a particular individual, group, institution, etc.

examples.x: She keeps her private thoughts and feelings to herself.
The company held a private meeting to discuss sensitive financial matters.
He owns a private yacht that he sails on weekends.
The school has private classrooms for tutoring sessions.
They rented a private cabin for their vacation in the mountains.

senses.definition: a school that receives money from the parents of the students instead of the government

examples.x: She attended a prestigious private school known for its excellent academic record.
The private school offers a wide range of extracurricular activities.
The cost of tuition at a private school can be quite high.
Many parents prefer sending their children to a private school for smaller class sizes.
Private schools often have more resources compared to public institutions.

senses.definition: a class or school that prepares four-year-old to six-year-old children for elementary school

examples.x: Kindergarten is often a child's first formal introduction to a structured learning environment, where they begin to develop essential social and academic skills.
The kindergarten curriculum typically includes activities that promote literacy, numeracy, and creativity, helping children to build a strong foundation for future learning.
Many parents choose to enroll their children in kindergarten programs that emphasize play-based learning, allowing kids to explore and discover through hands-on experiences.
During the kindergarten graduation ceremony, the young students proudly showcased their artwork and performed songs they had learned throughout the year.
Teachers in kindergarten play a vital role in fostering a love for learning, encouraging curiosity, and helping children develop important interpersonal skills through group activities.

senses.definition: someone who has received a university or college degree
to finish a university, college, etc. study course successfully and receive a diploma or degree

examples.x: She graduated with honors from the university.
They graduated from college with a degree in engineering.
They are excited to graduate and move on to the next phase of their lives.
She plans to graduate early so she can start her career.
He graduated at the top of his class in law school.

senses.definition: a student who is trying to complete their first degree in college or university

examples.x: Undergraduates typically pursue bachelor's degrees before considering graduate studies.
Many undergraduates take advantage of research opportunities to deepen their understanding of their field of study.

senses.definition: the action of successfully finishing studies at a high school or a university degree

examples.x: Graduation marks an important milestone in every student’s life.
The school held a party to celebrate the students’ graduation.
Graduation is often a time for reflecting on personal achievements.
The university organizes two graduations each year.
She felt proud to walk across the stage at her graduation.

senses.definition: the certificate that is given to university or college students upon successful completion of their course

examples.x: After years of hard work and dedication, she finally received her bachelor's degree in biology.
He proudly displayed his master's degree in engineering on the wall of his office.
Many employers require candidates to have at least a bachelor's degree in their field of expertise.
To enter the medical field, you must first obtain a medical degree.

senses.definition: a person who teaches courses at a college or university, often with a focus on undergraduate education, but who does not hold the rank of professor

examples.x: The lecturer explained the complex theory in simple terms.
She works as a lecturer in the Department of English at the university.
The new lecturer is introducing innovative teaching methods to the students.
The lecturer gave a fascinating lecture on Shakespeare’s works.
After completing her PhD, she became a lecturer in modern history.

senses.definition: someone who is receiving education, particularly a schoolchild

examples.x: Teacher assigned homework to each pupil in the class.
School organized a field trip to the museum, and the pupils were excited to explore.
The pupil's performance in mathematics improved significantly after receiving extra tutoring.
The school's policy requires pupils to wear uniforms as part of the dress code.

senses.definition: each part into which a day is divided at a school, university, etc.

examples.x: During the first period, the class focused on mathematics, tackling complex equations and problem-solving.
The teacher announced a surprise quiz during the third period, catching the students off guard.
In the afternoon period, the school offers elective courses such as art and music for students to explore their interests.

senses.definition: to study or practice taught lessons again, particularly to prepare for an examination

examples.x: With the final exam approaching, she set aside time each evening to review the course material.
He spent the weekend reviewing his notes and textbooks, determined to ace the upcoming chemistry test.
In order to pass the certification exam, he needs to review all the key concepts and formulas covered in the training course.
She likes to review her lecture notes right before bed.
The teacher encouraged the class to review their vocabulary flashcards regularly.

senses.definition: to go to school, university, church, etc. periodically

examples.x: Students are expected to attend all classes to ensure academic success.
He attended church every Sunday with his family.
The children attend school from Monday to Friday.
He attends a study group at the library every weekend.
They attend a music academy to learn to play instruments.

senses.definition: to stop going to school, university, or college before finishing one's studies

examples.x: Some students may choose to drop out if they find that their chosen course is not aligned with their interests.

senses.definition: to test a person's knowledge or skills in a certain subject by asking them questions or asking them to do a specific task

examples.x: The teacher will examine the students on their understanding of the lesson.
They will examine the candidates' problem-solving abilities during the interview.
The coach examined the players' techniques to improve their performance.
The examiner will examine your skills by asking you to complete a series of tasks.
He was examined on his ability to operate the equipment under pressure.

senses.definition: a series of questions in a book set to test one's knowledge or skill

examples.x: The math textbook included a variety of exercises at the end of each chapter to help students practice solving equations.
The language workbook contained exercises that focused on vocabulary, grammar, and comprehension to improve students' language skills.
As part of the science curriculum, students were assigned weekly lab exercises to conduct experiments and analyze results.

senses.definition: a formal written, practical, or spoken test used to assess someone's knowledge or skill in a specific subject or field

examples.x: She passed the practical examination required for her driver's license.
The examination tested both theoretical knowledge and practical skills.
To become certified, candidates must successfully complete a series of examinations.

senses.definition: a short test given to students

examples.x: The teacher gave a surprise quiz at the end of the lesson.
Students must complete the quiz within ten minutes.
She studied all night for the history quiz.
The quiz consisted of multiple-choice and short-answer questions.
He forgot about the quiz and had to guess most of the answers.

senses.definition: an amount of money that one pays to receive an education, particularly in a university or college
to study in a determined and serious manner

examples.x: They go to the beach when they should be hitting the books and then they wonder why they get bad grades.

senses.definition: someone who is very smart or is very skilled in a specific activity

examples.x: Albert Einstein is widely regarded as a genius in physics.
Her ability to solve complex problems so quickly proves she is a genius.
The young pianist was called a musical genius after his breathtaking performance.
The professor recognized the student’s hidden genius in mathematics.
Many consider Leonardo da Vinci a genius for his contributions to art and science.
